title: Redaction review
description: Verify personal data is removed or transformed before logs, errors, telemetry, support bundles, prompts, and exports.
body: Use for observability, debugging, support, analytics, model-context, or artifact changes. Do not rely solely on dashboard-level masking. Steps: identify producer sites, redact before emission, inspect normal and error outputs, test nested structures and arrays, and confirm correlation remains possible with non-identifying IDs. Validation signals include redacted snapshots and absence of personal fields in generated artifacts. Risk boundary: persisted raw personal data in logs requires human incident review. Use when Privacy Risk Controls work touches personal-data classes, sinks, retention, consent, logs, prompts, and exports. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: follow the ordered review, edit, and validation sequence; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include data inventory, redaction tests, retention checks, export/delete tests, and approval evidence. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves PII disclosure, consent bypass, retention drift, and unapproved processor expansion, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
